raceName: Ronde Van Brugge - Tour of Bruges
tldr: Dylan Groenewegen won the flat, wind-exposed Tour of Bruges (the successor to Classic Brugge-De Panne) ahead of Philipsen and Max Kanter — a pure sprinters' result on a course designed for it. Limited tactical narrative beyond the bunch sprint outcome.

narrative.headline: Groenewegen takes the Bruges bunch sprint
narrative.openingMoves: Flat coastal Belgian route. The race is the successor to Classic Brugge-De Panne; the format and the field both designed for sprinters.
narrative.raceUnfolds: Standard bunch-sprint Classics pattern. No major selection, no major splits in available coverage.
narrative.finale: Groenewegen took the line ahead of Philipsen and Kanter.

teamStories.narrative: Bruges was Groenewegen's first major spring win. The team's calendar through the cobbled block had been thin; this was the strongest opening day.
Philipsen was the favourite and finished second behind Groenewegen — clean second-place for the team's sprint card.

riderPerformances.name: Jasper Philipsen
riderPerformances.narrative: Second to Groenewegen in a flat coastal bunch sprint. Won Gent-Wevelgem four days later from the same kind of finish.

aftermath.headline: Sprint
aftermath.body: Tour of Bruges is the most reliable bunch-sprint day on the spring Belgian calendar. Groenewegen, Philipsen, Kanter is the kind of finish line you'd predict before the start.
